title: Junior Sales Manager/in – Inbound & Account Development (m/w/d)
_note: Formula score is high due to generic soft-skill requirements; core role is B2B inbound sales which is structurally outside Boru's marketing/PMM profile. Practical fit is moderate.
required_skills: Freude an strukturierter, nachhaltiger Vertriebsarbeit mit Fokus auf Beziehungsmanagement
Ausgeprägtes Gespür für Kundenbedarfe und die Fähigkeit, komplexe Lösungen verständlich zu vermitteln
Bereitschaft, technisches Know-how über das Produkt aufzubauen
Strategisches Denken mit Blick auf langfristige Kundenentwicklung
Exzellente Kommunikationsfähigkeiten und Authentizität im Umgang mit Kunden
Souveränes Auftreten auf Deutsch und Englisch
bonus_skills: Erste Erfahrung im B2B-Vertrieb, Account Management oder Customer Success – idealerweise im SaaS-Umfeld
matched_skills: Selbstständige strukturierte Arbeitsweise — evidenced across PMO (HSBC), project coordination (Hapag-Lloyd), and GTM ownership (IPC, GRG)
Komplexe Lösungen verständlich vermitteln — IPC IoT product positioning, argumentation guides, and live B2B demos to 100+ clients at trade fairs (GRG)
Technisches Know-how aufbauen — IoT/IT sector background at IPC; FinTech digital transformation at HSBC
Strategisches Denken Kundenentwicklung — B2B GTM strategy across 4 product launches (IPC), portfolio gap analysis (GRG), competitive intelligence (ECE)
Kommunikationsfähigkeit — C-suite reporting (HSBC), multi-stakeholder coordination, sales enablement materials
Teamplayer — cross-functional collaboration in all roles (HSBC 6 workstreams, Hapag-Lloyd agile teams)
missing_skills: B2B-Vertrieb / Account Management Erfahrung — Boru's background is product marketing and PMO, not direct sales; GRG pre-sales support is closest but title was Product Marketing Manager
Freude an Vertriebsarbeit — no demonstrated sales career trajectory; CV is marketing/PMM focused
Deutsch souverän (implizit C1) — CV states B1+, insufficient for client-facing German-language sales role
CRM-System management (Salesforce/HubSpot pipeline forecasting) — not mentioned in CV
Inbound lead qualification experience — no direct evidence of owning a sales funnel
core_responsibilities: Strukturierte Bearbeitung und Qualifizierung eingehender Leads (Inbound) von der Anfrage bis zur Conversion
Ganzheitliches Account Management bestehender Kunden — Aufbau vertrauensvoller Kundenbeziehungen
Bedarfsanalyse und Identifikation von Cross-/Upselling-Potenzialen für eIDAS/Signatur-Lösungen
CRM-Pflege inkl. Forecasting und KPI-Reporting (Pipeline, Bestandskunden, Verlängerungen)
Enge Zusammenarbeit mit Sales, Produktmanagement, Technical Customer Success und Marketing
recommended_emphasis: Lead with GRG pre-sales: 'Delivered product demonstrations and pre-sales support to 100+ B2B clients at trade fairs; coordinated lead capture supporting the sales pipeline' — this is the closest analogue to inbound account development

Frame IPC GTM work as sales-adjacent: B2B GTM strategy across 4 product launches including channel partner management, SOPs, and sales training materials — positions Boru as someone who has bridged marketing and sales
Highlight HSBC cross-functional influencing without authority (Product, Engineering, Marketing, C-suite) as evidence of stakeholder/relationship management capability relevant to account development
Reference IPC and GRG trade fair experience directly — the JD explicitly calls out Messe-Support as a core responsibility

Acknowledge the marketing-to-sales pivot in the cover letter; frame it as adding a strategic dimension to sales that a pure sales hire wouldn't bring (customer insight, positioning, data storytelling)
summary: Formula score of 76.0 is inflated by strong generic skill matches (communication, structured work, strategic thinking, technical learning) but masks a structural gap: this is an inbound B2B sales and account management role while Boru's profile is product marketing and PMO. The closest relevant experience is GRG's pre-sales support (B2B demos to 100+ clients) and IPC's channel partner management — both sales-adjacent but from a marketing seat. Hard gaps are German language fluency (B1+ vs. implied C1 for client-facing German role) and no CRM/pipeline ownership history. Worth applying only if Boru is deliberately pivoting toward sales/account management; the cover letter must proactively address the marketing-to-sales transition and position it as a strength.
